mission.title: redpanda: Improve full disk handling
mission.description: Improve full disk handling. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current redpanda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your redpanda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
helm list -n redpanda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working redpanda installation.

steps.title: Review redpanda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant redpanda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get configmap -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=redpanda
```
Currently, when a disk fills up we assert out.  This is obviously not that slick, but to be more specific on the impact:
- In >3 node clusters, nodes will tend to have asymmetric disk use.  The unlucky node with the most bytes will terminate first.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Improve full disk handling
steps.description:
Assuming this is the assert you are talking about?

```
Oct 17 05:00:27 ip-172-31-20-72 rpk[88172]: ERROR 2021-10-17 05:00:27,468 [shard 0] assert - Assert failure: (../../../src/v/storage/segment_appender.cc:346) 'false' We failed to fallocate file. This usually means we have ran out of disk space. Please check your data partition and ensure you have enough space. Error: std::__1::system_error (error system:28, No>
Oct 17 05:00:27 ip-172-31-20-72 rpk[88172]: ERROR 2021-10-17 05:00:27,468

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get events -n redpanda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Improve full disk handling" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Assuming this is the assert you are talking about?

```
Oct 17 05:00:27 ip-172-31-20-72 rpk[88172]: ERROR 2021-10-17 05:00:27,468 [shard 0] assert - Assert failure: (../../../src/v/storage/segment_appender.cc:346) 'false' We failed to fallocate file. This usually means we have ran out of disk space. Please check your data partition and ensure you have enough space.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with redpanda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
